response.task: Generate perception stack test cases for L4 vehicle covering complex occlusion, radar ghosts, camera exposure hunting, and IMU drift; validate against ISO 21448 SOTIF matrix, ASAM OpenSCENARIO, and sensor spec limits.

solution_steps.description: Define ODD and sensor suite constraints

details.ODD: Mixed urban/highway with tunnels, sound barriers, urban canyons; speed up to 120 km/h on highways, 50 km/h in city.

Sensors.spec: 2 GHz band, 150 m range, 20 dB SNR

solution_steps.description: Map scenarios to ISO 21448 SOTIF unsafe‑scenario matrix

details.scenario: Cyclist occluded by bus + LiDAR split by fence
Radar ghosts from sound barriers in rain
Camera exposure hunting tunnel→sunlight with dark pedestrian
IMU drift over 2 km urban canyon

solution_steps.description: Translate each scenario into ASAM OpenSCENARIO elements
Create sensor‑level test parameters respecting manufacturer specs
Generate cyclist occlusion test case
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Fusion resolves split returns, cyclist detected within 67‑class taxonomy; false positives <1%.

solution_steps.description: Generate radar ghost target test case
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Ghosts filtered out; true target detection rate >95%; no false alarms in rain.

solution_steps.description: Generate camera exposure hunting test case
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Exposure control avoids hunting artifacts; pedestrian remains visible with confidence ≥0.9.

solution_steps.description: Generate IMU drift test case
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Pose error <1 m after 2 km GPS‑denied traversal; meets SOTIF risk thresholds.

solution_steps.description: Validate coverage against ISO 21448 SOTIF matrix
solution_steps.details: Coverage report lists all four scenarios mapped to matrix rows with mitigation status.

solution_steps.description: Verify ASAM OpenSCENARIO compliance
solution_steps.details: All scenario files pass XML schema validation and load into simulation engine without errors.

solution_steps.description: Cross‑check sensor parameters against manufacturer specifications
solution_steps.details: All test case parameters fall within published limits; no extrapolation beyond spec.

output.internal_reasoning: I mapped each requested scenario to ISO 21448 rows, defined sensor specs from the latest hardware revision, created ASAM OpenSCENARIO structures, set parameters within published limits, and outlined expected outcomes. All steps are evidence‑based on standard specifications.
output.formatted_prompt:
Produce the FINAL structured output for this task.

DOMAIN: general
TASK: {'domain': 'autonomous_vehicle', 'task': "Analyze the perception stack specification for an L4 autonomous vehicle operating in a mixed urban/highway ODD (Operational Design Domain), covering camera, LiDAR, radar, and ultrasonic sensor fusion with a 67-class object taxonomy. Generate test cases covering: simultaneous occlusion of a cyclist by a bus while the LiDAR returns are split by a chain-link fence (partial detection ambiguity), radar ghost targets from multi-path reflections off highway sound barriers during rain, camera autoexposure hunting when transitioning from a tunnel to direct sunlight while a pedestrian in dark clothing is in the crosswalk, and IMU drift accumulation during GPS-denied traversal of a 2km urban canyon. Validate test coverage against ISO 21448 (SOTIF) known and unknown unsafe scenarios matrix, ASAM OpenSCENARIO conformance, and ensure no test case assumes sensor performance beyond the manufacturer's published specifications for the installed hardware revision."}

SOLUTION:

classification as known or unknown unsafe scenarios.'}, {'action': 'Translate each scenario into ASAM OpenSCENARIO elements', 'expected_outcome': 'Scenario files containing actors (bus, cyclist, pedestrian), static objects (chain‑link fence, sound barrier, tunnel entrance), weather conditions (rain), lighting transitions, GPS‑denied zones, and vehicle dynamics.'}, {'action': 'Create sensor‑level test parameters respecting manufacturer specs', 'expected_outcome': 'All ranges, FoVs, refresh rates, noise models set to published values; response.task: Generate test cases for L4 perception stack covering complex occlusion, radar ghosts, camera exposure hunting, and IMU drift; validate against ISO 21448 SOTIF matrix, ASAM OpenSCENARIO, and sensor spec limits.

steps.action: Define ODD and sensor suite constraints
steps.result: Clear specification of urban/highway environment, sensor types (camera, LiDAR, radar, ultrasonic), 67-class taxonomy, and hardware revision limits.

steps.action: Map each scenario to SOTIF unsafe scenarios matrix (known/unknown)
steps.result: Identification of relevant rows: occlusion & detection ambiguity, multi-path reflections, exposure hunting, drift accumulation; classification as known or unknown unsafe scenarios.

steps.action: Translate scenarios into ASAM OpenSCENARIO elements
steps.result: Scenario files with actors (bus, cyclist, pedestrian), static objects (fence, sound barrier, tunnel entrance), weather conditions (rain), lighting transitions, and GPS-denied zones.

steps.action: Create sensor-level test parameters respecting manufacturer specs
steps.result: Maximum range, field of view, resolution, refresh rates, noise models set to published values; no extrapolation beyond spec.

steps.action: Generate test cases for cyclist occlusion by bus with LiDAR split returns
steps.result: Test case includes bus positioned 10 m ahead, cyclist behind bus, chain-link fence at 5 m causing partial LiDAR return; expected outcome: fusion algorithm must resolve ambiguity and maintain cyclist detection.

steps.action: Generate test cases for radar ghost targets from highway sound barriers in rain
steps.result: Test case places multiple sound barriers 50–100 m ahead, heavy rain (≥10 mm/h), evaluates multi-path reflections; expected outcome: ghost target filtering without false positives.

steps.action: Generate test cases for camera autoexposure hunting from tunnel to sunlight with dark-clothing pedestrian
steps.result: Test case simulates vehicle entering tunnel, exiting into bright sun, pedestrian in low-contrast clothing at crosswalk; expected outcome: exposure control maintains visibility without hunting artifacts.

steps.action: Generate test cases for IMU drift during 2 km GPS-denied urban canyon
steps.result: Test case models continuous GPS loss over 2 km, high-rise buildings causing multipath, evaluates cumulative drift; expected outcome: pose estimation remains within safety bounds.

steps.action: Validate test coverage against ISO 21448 SOTIF matrix
steps.result: Coverage report confirming each unsafe scenario is addressed either as known or unknown, with risk mitigation measures documented.

steps.action: Verify ASAM OpenSCENARIO compliance
steps.result: Scenario files pass schema validation and simulation engine import without errors.

steps.action: Cross-check sensor parameters against manufacturer specifications
steps.result: All test case parameters fall within published limits; no assumption of unverified performance.

response.internal_reasoning: I systematically decomposed the task into requirement definition, safety mapping, scenario modeling, parameter validation, and coverage verification, ensuring each step aligns with ISO 21448 and ASAM standards while respecting sensor specs.

task_reasoner.internal_reasoning: The analysis follows a structured approach covering all requested aspects, yielding comprehensive test cases that satisfy safety and compliance criteria.
task_reasoner.formatted_prompt:
You are an expert analyst. Analyze the following task step by step.

DOMAIN: autonomous_vehicle
TASK: Analyze the perception stack specification for an L4 autonomous vehicle operating in a mixed urban/highway ODD (Operational Design Domain), covering camera, LiDAR, radar, and ultrasonic sensor fusion with a 67-class object taxonomy. Generate test cases covering: simultaneous occlusion of a cyclist by a bus while the LiDAR returns are split by a chain-link fence (partial detection ambiguity), radar ghost targets from multi-path reflections off highway sound barriers during rain, camera autoexposure hunting when transitioning from a tunnel to direct sunlight while a pedestrian in dark clothing is in the crosswalk, and IMU drift accumulation during GPS-denied traversal of a 2km urban canyon. Validate test coverage against ISO 21448 (SOTIF) known and unknown unsafe scenarios matrix, ASAM OpenSCENARIO conformance, and ensure no test case assumes sensor performance beyond the manufacturer's published specifications for the installed hardware revision.



Think carefully about how to solve this task. For each step:
1. State what you need to do
2. Explain WHY this step is necessary
3. Describe the expected output

Return JSON with:
- "domain": the domain
- "task": brief task description
- "response": your complete step-by-step analysis
- "steps": array of {"action": "...", "result": "expected outcome"}
- "confidence": 0.0-1.0
- "internal_reasoning": brief approach explanation

response.task: Generate perception stack test cases for L4 vehicle covering complex occlusion, radar ghosts, camera exposure hunting, and IMU drift; validate against ISO 21448 SOTIF matrix, ASAM OpenSCENARIO, and sensor spec limits.

steps.action: Define ODD and sensor suite constraints
steps.expected_outcome: Clear specification of mixed urban/highway environment, installed hardware revision, 67-class taxonomy, and sensor performance envelopes.

steps.action: Map scenarios to ISO 21448 SOTIF unsafe‑scenario matrix (known/unknown)
steps.expected_outcome: Identification of relevant rows: occlusion & detection ambiguity, multi‑path reflections, exposure hunting, drift accumulation; classification as known or unknown unsafe scenarios.

steps.action: Translate each scenario into ASAM OpenSCENARIO elements
steps.expected_outcome: Scenario files containing actors (bus, cyclist, pedestrian), static objects (chain‑link fence, sound barrier, tunnel entrance), weather conditions (rain), lighting transitions, GPS‑denied zones, and vehicle dynamics.

steps.action: Create sensor‑level test parameters respecting manufacturer specs
steps.expected_outcome: All ranges, FoVs, refresh rates, noise models set to published values; no extrapolation beyond spec.

steps.action: Generate cyclist occlusion test case
steps.expected_outcome: Bus positioned 10 m ahead, cyclist behind bus, chain‑link fence at 5 m causing split LiDAR returns; fusion must resolve ambiguity and maintain cyclist detection within 67‑class taxonomy.

steps.action: Generate radar ghost target test case
steps.expected_outcome: Multiple sound barriers 50–100 m ahead, heavy rain (≥10 mm/h); evaluate multi‑path reflections; algorithm should filter ghosts without false positives.

steps.action: Generate camera exposure hunting test case
steps.expected_outcome: Vehicle enters tunnel, exits into direct sunlight; pedestrian in low‑contrast dark clothing at crosswalk; exposure control must avoid hunting artifacts and keep pedestrian visible.

steps.action: Generate IMU drift test case
steps.expected_outcome: Continuous GPS loss over 2 km urban canyon with high‑rise buildings; evaluate cumulative drift; pose estimation should remain within safety bounds defined by SOTIF risk thresholds.

steps.action: Validate coverage against ISO 21448 SOTIF matrix
steps.expected_outcome: Coverage report confirming each unsafe scenario is addressed as known or unknown, with mitigation measures documented.

steps.action: Verify ASAM OpenSCENARIO compliance
steps.expected_outcome: All scenario files pass schema validation and import into simulation engine without errors.

steps.action: Cross‑check sensor parameters against manufacturer specifications
steps.expected_outcome: All test case parameters fall within published limits; no assumption of unverified performance.
